description_long: This object is a pair of locking pliers, commonly known as Vise-Grips, constructed primarily from metal with a dark, possibly blackened or coated finish. The tool features two curved handles, one of which incorporates an adjustment screw at the end for setting the jaw width and locking force. The jaws are robust and slightly curved, designed to grip objects securely, and are connected by a central pivot mechanism that allows the handles to clamp together with significant force. The surface of the handles appears smooth and metallic, lacking any visible rubberized or ergonomic padding. The overall build is solid and utilitarian, suitable for gripping, twisting, or holding objects in place during repairs or construction. No visible branding or logos are present on the exterior surfaces.
description: A sturdy, dark-finished pair of locking pliers with curved handles, an adjustment screw, and robust, slightly curved jaws for gripping objects securely.
description_view_0: Front view showing the curved jaws and the adjustment screw at the end of one handle.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the handles and the alignment of the jaws.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the jaws and the smooth, unpadded handles.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the adjustment screw and the curvature of the handles.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, smooth metal handles without insulation or ergonomic features.
attribute_jaw_design: Curved, robust jaws with a locking mechanism; likely serrated for enhanced grip, typical of locking pliers.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Compound pivot with a central joint and an adjustable screw for locking and jaw width adjustment.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with no visible texturing or pattern.
